title: Cross-sectional area
short: The area of a wire's end face, measured in square metres (m²).
full:
The cross-sectional area S is the area of the slice you would see by cutting a wire straight across. A thicker wire (larger S) gives charge more room to flow and so has a lower resistance, R = ρ·L/S — the area is in the denominator.

For a round wire of radius r, S = π·r². The SI unit is the square metre (m²).
